Urobilinogen in Urine 
URL of this page: https://medlineplus.gov/lab-tests/urobilinogen-in-urine/ 
Urobilinogen in Urine 
To use the sharing features on this page, please enable JavaScript. 
What is a urobilinogen in urine test? 
A urobilinogen in urine test measures the amount of urobilinogen in your urine (pee). Urobilinogen comes from bilirubin. Your body makes bilirubin during the normal process of breaking down old red blood cells. Your liver uses the bilirubin to make bile, a fluid that helps you digest food in your intestines.
